reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to use the workflow: first PLAN, then review_plan, then APPLY.

We are given a repair: disable kernel parameter for accepting ICMP redirects by default on IPv6, i.e., set net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects=0. Setting runtime: sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects=0. Persistence: add line to /etc/sysctl.d/ file. Also comment out any existing occurrences. Also need to handle /etc/sysctl.conf file. Need to use review_plan tool first. Provide plan description. Then after approval, run commands.

We have review feedback requiring improvements. Should adhere to policy: I will deliver plan accordingly.

We'll produce plan: read /etc/sysctl.conf, any un-commented setting net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects should be commented out. Same for /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf and /run/sysctl.d/*.conf and /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf. After commenting out, add new file /etc/sysctl.d/99-custom-ipv6-accept-redirects.conf or similar with line net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0. Ensure file permission root:root 644. Then apply runtime with sysctl -w. Then run sysctl --system to apply all settings. Use sed to comment, but need to avoid commented lines or lines that contain key in comments only. Use regex: '^\s*net\.ipv6\.conf\.default\.accept_redirects\s*=' maybe anchored. If matches, change to start with '#'. Also ensure we don't match lines commented out (#). Should check for lines that already have value 0. But we comment out anyway. That is fine but we could skip comment if it's already commented? Well comment logic should skip commented lines. So regex to match lines: '^\s*net\.ipv6\.conf\.default\.accept_redirects\s*=' ignoring leading whitespace and not starting with '#'.

But we might want to comment using sed: sed -i 's/^\(\s*net\.ipv6\.conf\.default\.accept_redirects\s*=\s*\).*$/# \1$2/' can't be done? Actually we can capture. Or we use sed: sed -i 's/^\(\s*net\.ipv6\.conf\.default\.accept_redirects\s*=\s*\).*$/# \1/'; but keep value? Let's think: We want to add a comment '#' at beginning and optionally keep the line as a comment. Use sed "/^\s*net\.ipv6\.conf\.default\.accept_redirects\s*=/s/^/# /". That comments whole line.

But we need to apply to each config file path. Approach: for each file path, read_file. Use sed -i with that command. But we must be careful not to modify symlinks incorrectly. If /etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf is symlink to /etc/sysctl.conf, we should skip? Actually requirement: "skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf)", So we need to check readlink -f. But to avoid complexity for this environment? In our plan, we can specify to skip symlink by verifying file path after readlink. Use readlink -f to compare to /etc/sysctl.conf.

Given we are not executing complex scripts according to rules; we must run one command at a time and not chain. That may be limiting. But the plan may involve multiple commands like read_file, then run sed, etc. Possibly we need to script logic across multiple steps manually: use read_file to fetch content then parse and create new content with corrected lines, write_file. But we can't run loops in one command; we must issue commands sequentially.

We'll produce plan: read /etc/sysctl.conf and check if any active setting for accept_redirects. If found, modify to comment it out. We'll do read_file, analyze via shell content, then run sed or write updated content. But commands must be run as root, one at a time. Probably easiest: Using sed in one command to comment out the line in /etc/sysctl.conf. This is one command operation per file.

Similarly for other conf files: collect list of relevant files, for each file, use sed. But we don't want to run many commands in single script. Could run sed commands for each file separately. But we can't chain them, each must be separate. That's okay.

We also need to add new file /etc/sysctl.d/99-ipv6-accept-redirects.conf containing net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0. Use write_file. Also set mode 644, permissions root:root. We can use write_file tool with mode: "0644".

Then run sysctl -w to set runtime. Then run sysctl --system.

Now we need to propose plan using run_cmd. Use read_file to inspect files if they exist but to be safe we can just run sed -i to comment out any matching lines; if the file does not exist, sed will fail, but that's fine. Use 'run_cmd' to execute 'sed -i ... file'.

However, we must avoid duplicating entries and maintain idempotency. The sed regex should match lines not already commented (#) starting with optional whitespace and pattern. Good.

Now produce plan.

We'll produce plan with steps.

After that, we call review_plan.
